scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) delegates to treeBuilder.parse and returns the Document when html and baseUri are non-null
scenarios.precondition: Use a stub TreeBuilder whose parse(input, baseUri, parser) returns a dummy Document instance; real Parser initialized with stub builder
scenarios.expectedResult: the same dummy Document instance returned by the stub TreeBuilder.parse
pseudocode: TEST TC01 parseInput over String overload normal

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) delegates to treeBuilder.parse and returns the Document when reader and baseUri are non-null
scenarios.precondition: Use a stub TreeBuilder whose parse(reader, baseUri, parser) returns a dummyDoc; real StringReader wraps input
scenarios.expectedResult: the same dummyDoc returned by the stub TreeBuilder.parse
pseudocode: TEST TC02 parseInput over Reader overload normal
Reader input = new StringReader("<div/>"); String baseUri = "base";

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with null html should throw NullPointerException at StringReader construction
pseudocode: TEST TC03 parseInput null-html throws NPE

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with null baseUri should cause treeBuilder.parse to throw IllegalArgumentException
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that throws IllegalArgumentException if baseUri is null; document stub not used
pseudocode: TEST TC04 parseInput null-baseUri throws IAE
Parser parser = new Parser(stubBuilder(throw IAE if baseUri null));

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) with null Reader should throw NullPointerException from treeBuilder.parse stub
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that throws NullPointerException when reader is null
pseudocode: TEST TC05 parseInput null-Reader throws NPE
Parser parser = new Parser(stubBuilder(throw NPE if reader null));

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) when treeBuilder.parse throws custom RuntimeException propagates the exception
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that throws new IllegalStateException("err") unconditionally
pseudocode: TEST TC06 parseInput Reader overload propagates runtime-exception
Reader input = new StringReader("x"); String baseUri = "b";

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with empty html string returns a Document (empty-parsed) from stub
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that returns emptyDoc when html is empty string
pseudocode: TEST TC07 parseInput empty-html returns emptyDoc
Parser parser = new Parser(stubBuilder(returning emptyDoc on empty input));

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with large html string is passed through to treeBuilder.parse once
scenarios.precondition: Use a real HtmlTreeBuilder to parse a long repeated fragment deterministically
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document.body().childNodeSize() equals N where N matches number of top-level elements
pseudocode: TEST TC08 parseInput large-html real parsing

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with html containing script tag ensures treeBuilder.parse recognizes script content
pseudocode: TEST TC09 parseInput html-with-script real parsing

scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with position tracking enabled does not affect returned Document structure
scenarios.precondition: Use real HtmlTreeBuilder; call parser.setTrackPosition(true) before parsing
scenarios.expectedResult: Document structure identical to non-tracked parsing (body().childNodeSize()>0 for input)
pseudocode: TEST TC10 parseInput with position tracking enabled
